player_position.current_location: Tessara Port — Pier Quarter (Cup plaza lane)

campaign_rules.player_verb_single: FINISHER COMMIT only. Orders / acceptances / refusals are declared intent.
campaign_rules.day_11_attack_ceiling: CONQUEROR'S WILL (heavy tier). DIVINE SUNDERER does NOT fire at Tessara.
campaign_rules.destruction_tier_cap_tessara: wall cracked / rail splintered / floor showed the strike — no building collapse, no island-topography change
campaign_rules.opponent_tier_cap_tessara: Commodore at maximum (Hudore in the Cup final only). No Vice Admiral / Admiral / Yonko / Warlord present.
campaign_rules.post_peak_collapse_rule: Mandatory body receipt after every named-attack / Haki burst / ideology-fight peak. Default tier at Day 11 = WINDED-to-TIRED. SPENT/BROKEN require saga weight.

stat_consequences.event: The Golgatha is visibly damaged. Merchants avoid the pier. Crew morale drops.
The Golgatha is barely seaworthy. The Twenty are patching in shifts.
The crew is thin. Mako's visible-fear barometer tight. Feasts, comedy beats, or a real win needed.
The crew is loud. Feast energy. Volo commits the crew to three more things before sundown.

rules: Haki stats advance ONLY via world-earned beats — first Conqueror's burst, first Observation precog save, first Armament break of a Haki-coated shell. DM narrates the event, then ticks the stat. Not time-based.
Bounty is set by the world AFTER a public arc closes — not ticked, not estimated. Stays at current value until a filed event produces a new number.
Ship hull damage is set by combat/storm events. Repair is scene-based (Darius's ironwood contract holds until end-of-arc-close).
Crew morale ticks are scene-driven: feast +10, nakama beat +5, loss-of-crewmate -20, loss-of-arc -10, public win +15. DM updates at scene-close via dm-survival.sh custom-stat <name> +/-amount.
Inventory is narrative texture, not grid-constrained. Merdo carries what he carries.
Unique items (Judgment, Gnadenkette, Mama+Papa, Higure, Shibito-Bashi, Yann's fruit basket, Darius's ironwood scrap, Rook's sealed letter) are always tracked.
Stackable goods (berries-currency, food, ship supplies, repair material) are quantity-tracked but not slotted.
